topic: Verification of c5 H1-H5 patches: 'unpublished'→W004 baseline, defender ensemble upper-bound, 'inversely' removed, magnetometer station type, sentence split
our_argument_summary: SEC-6.14 vise paragraph (part6.html ~108,900–113,500) now (a) names the dome's published W004 baseline (−22.24 nT) as the implicit operational baseline behind the 10–13 µGal target, with per-station BOU/W004 numbers cross-walked through κ to 3.0–7.6 µGal and 6.1–15.6 µGal respectively; (b) demonstrates the ensemble-bounded defender retreat using the Kim & Chang 2018 upper edge (ΔB=15 nT → Δg=8.94 µGal, ~1 µGal short of the 10 µGal lower target); (c) drops the 'inversely' descriptor and clarifies station class as 'magnetometer'; (d) splits the long lead sentence into a claim + arithmetic parenthetical. The structural vise (either κ applies generally and 1999/2009 SG nulls refute it, or κ has scope conditions that destroy PRED-TIER-3) is preserved; the patches strengthen rather than dilute that argument by trading 'unpublished and therefore unverifiable' for 'published as W004 and therefore must be defended on the merits.'

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The patched text relies on dome-published per-station ΔB predictions under BOU/W004 baselines and the dome-published κ=1.677 nT/µGal coupling. All arithmetic verified against raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 23, 26–34.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Validation is mixed: the dome publishes the inputs (baselines + per-station predictions + κ); the section's back-calc Δg = ΔB/κ is purely arithmetic from those inputs and matches raw-text values to within 0.05 µGal.
data_sources_used: Kim & Chang 2018 Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049 DOI:10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 (5–15 nT ensemble)
Mansinha et al. 2001 J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) DOI:10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387 (1999 Membach SG null)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The patched paragraph explicitly concedes the ensemble-upper-edge defender retreat (8.94 µGal at 15 nT upper edge) and notes the live ensemble-bounded defense; this is adversarial discipline rather than an escape clause for the dome.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by the H1-H5 patches; pre-existing PRED-TIER-3 vs dome's E-PRED-C (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:39 — 'SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal') distinction remains unaddressed in section text, but is out of scope for this verification.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: SEC-6.14 references the dome's published per-station ΔB predictions (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:23–34) and Mansinha 2001 SG null reanalysis. The actual prediction values are live-published on dome predictions.html and tracked by our poller, not statically baked into our section. Tagging at section level applies the same convention as the underlying WIN-013/014 evidence.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The κ coupling claim is dome-specific; no standard-physics analog exists for a magnetometer→SG coupling constant of 1.677 nT/µGal applied across distinct eclipse events. Section is critiquing the dome's invented coupling, not a relabeled standard mechanism.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: κ is computed from Wang et al. 2000 (Mohe 1997, denominator) and BOU 2017 (numerator) — both observations long predate the dome's adoption of κ in its current quantitative form. The dome did not predict 1.677 nT/µGal in advance; it computed the ratio after both eclipses were measured.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: κ = 10.9 nT / 6.5 µGal = 1.677 is a single ratio of two observations from two different eclipses on two different continents using two different instrument types. There is no derivation from dome geometric parameters (disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, etc.) that produces 1.677 nT/µGal.

holes_found.description: UNIT-CITATION ERROR in the ensemble-bounded defender pivot. The patched H2 text states '8.94 µGal vs 10 µGal is within the ±2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands the dome publishes.' Cross-check of raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 26–34 shows the dome publishes per-station uncertainties as ±1.0 to ±2.6 nT (magnetic side), not µGal (gravity side). Propagating ±2 nT through κ (treating κ as known) yields ±2/1.677 ≈ ±1.19 µGal on the inferred Δg, not ±2 µGal. The 1.06 µGal shortfall (10 − 8.94) is therefore narrowly inside the propagated band (~±1.19 µGal) — the patch's argument structure is salvageable — but the stated '±2 µGal' overstates the dome's published uncertainty by ~68% on the gravity side. A defender will catch this: 'You said our ±2 µGal uncertainty bands — but our published bands are ±2 nT, which through your own κ propagates to ±1.19 µGal. Pick units.' The fix is small but the citation must be honest.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace '±2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands the dome publishes' with either (a) '±1.2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands (propagating the dome's ±2 nT magnetic bands through κ=1.677 nT/µGal)' — explicit and pedantically correct, or (b) '±2 nT per-station uncertainty bands the dome publishes, which through κ propagate to ±1.19 µGal' — keeps the original nT framing visible and shows the propagation step. Either replacement preserves the 'ensemble-bounded defense is live' conclusion because 1.06 µGal < 1.19 µGal still holds. The current phrasing has the right qualitative answer but with a unit-confusion that a careful defender will turn into an own-goal.

holes_found.description: 'high-coverage central stations' terminology is loose. The patch identifies Ebro (0.95), San Pablo (0.90), Coimbra (0.92), Chambon (0.70), and Hartland (0.80) as 'high-coverage central stations' that land in or above 10–13 µGal under W004. The 0.90+ coverages (Ebro, San Pablo, Coimbra) are unambiguously high; Hartland (0.80) is mid-high; Chambon (0.70) is medium and is below the gap between selected and excluded stations (Canary 0.72 is excluded). The selection criterion is roughly 'coverage ≥ 0.70 AND geographically central along the path' but neither criterion is stated explicitly. Defender retreat: 'Why is 0.70 the threshold? Lerwick at 0.42 and Niemegk at 0.40 are exactly the path-marginal stations whose lower predicted Δg would dilute your claim — your selection criterion looks tuned to the conclusion.'
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-clause clarification. Replace 'the high-coverage central stations (Ebro, San Pablo, Coimbra, Chambon, Hartland)' with 'the five mid-to-high coverage central-path stations (Ebro 0.95, San Pablo 0.90, Coimbra 0.92, Hartland 0.80, Chambon 0.70 — i.e., all stations with eclipse coverage ≥0.70)'. Names the threshold explicitly, drops the soft 'central' qualifier in favor of the verifiable coverage cutoff. ~20-word add.

holes_found.description: Vise paragraph density. The 'vise' paragraph (part6.html ~108,950 to ~113,300) is now ~600 words spanning the original two-horned-choice argument PLUS the new ensemble-upper-edge concession PLUS the new W004 baseline walkthrough PLUS the closing 'Both horns lose at least one WIN' anchor. The original H5 patch (sentence split for the 16.8–21.8 nT lead) improved local readability inside the paragraph, but the paragraph as a whole is now markedly longer and denser than its neighbors in §6.14. A first-time reader may lose the structural argument under the volume of arithmetic. The c5 H5 spirit (improve readability) is partially undone by the larger H1 W004-baseline expansion.
holes_found.recommendation:
Optional paragraph break. Split the vise paragraph after 'so the ensemble-bounded defense is live and the dome can credibly argue the shortfall disappears under station-level uncertainty.' The first paragraph then carries the structural two-horned vise + the ensemble-upper-edge concession; the second paragraph carries the W004-baseline walkthrough + the 'vise tightens' closing. The two paragraphs would be ~280 + ~320 words — both still long but each carries one coherent sub-argument. No content change, just one

insertion.

stronger_arguments.description: The W004 baseline disclosure has a stronger framing available than the current 'is the W004 premise defensible' question. W004 is named after a single eclipse event (per the patch text 'derived from a single eclipse event, not from the 207-measurement global ensemble'). The dome publishes W004 to four-decimal-precision (−22.24 nT) — implying a single-event measurement is being treated as a model parameter to ~0.05 nT resolution. This is *itself* a methodological inconsistency the section could flag: a single measurement does not justify four-significant-figure precision when the 207-measurement ensemble gives 5–15 nT (one-significant-figure resolution at best). The vise could close on 'W004 is published, defensible-on-the-merits, AND over-precise relative to its single-event empirical basis.'
sources: raw-text/03-predictions.txt:23 (W004 empirical = −22.24 nT, no measurement-uncertainty band on the baseline itself)
Kim & Chang 2018 (5–15 nT ensemble, ~10 nT typical resolution at totality)

stronger_arguments.description: PRED-TIER-3 vs dome's E-PRED-C distinction. The dome's predictions.html (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:39) explicitly publishes 'E-PRED-C — SG gravimeters 0.0 uGal' as one of its 4 Model-Discriminating Tests for August 2026. The section currently treats PRED-TIER-3 (our internal taxonomy, claim: '10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals' per data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3) as the dome's headline SG prediction. A defender can read these as compatible — 'standard SG output 0.0 µGal, Level-3 residual reanalysis 10–13 µGal' — but the section text does not acknowledge the Level-3 residual qualifier. A future patch should distinguish 'standard SG output' (where the dome predicts 0.0 µGal and the 1999/2009 nulls match) from 'Level-3 residual reanalysis' (the dome's 10–13 µGal target). Pre-existing across multiple cycles; not introduced by the H1-H5 patches but worth flagging here as it weakens the 'past SG nulls refute κ' argument under the most charitable read of PRED-TIER-3.
sources: data/predictions.json:PRED-TIER-3 ('SG contact-phase κ test, 10–13 µGal in Level-3 residuals')

kernel_of_truth.description: The patches correctly identify and credit the dome's quantitative August 2026 disclosure (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:23–34 — 9 stations, two baselines, per-station uncertainty bands). This is real adversarial discipline: the c5 review's 'unpublished' framing was wrong (the dome IS publishing the per-station ΔB predictions); the patch's W004-baseline framing is the honest version. The patched paragraph also correctly concedes the ensemble-upper-edge defender retreat (8.94 µGal at ΔB=15 nT) rather than under-stating it as the c5 'typical-ensemble 10-13 nT' example did. Across H1–H5 the patches collectively trade rhetorical strength for factual accuracy — the right trade.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Even with the H1-H5 patches landed, the κ structural argument is unchanged: the dome's own κ=1.677 nT/µGal coupling predicted several-µGal SG pulses at Membach 1999 and the Chinese SG network 2009 that did not appear; either κ applies generally (refuted) or κ has scope conditions (PRED-TIER-3 destroyed). The W004-baseline walkthrough makes the implicit dome commitment explicit but does not produce a derivation from dome geometry to 10–13 µGal; W004 remains a single-event empirical baseline, not a model output. The patches improve the argument's epistemics without changing its conclusion.

text_fingerprint.verdict_anchor: Both horns lose at least one WIN

deeper_analysis: All five c5 patches (H1 'unpublished'→W004; H2 typical-ensemble→ensemble upper edge 15 nT/8.94 µGal; H3 'inversely' removed; H4 'named target stations'→'named magnetometer stations'; H5 long sentence split into claim + arithmetic parenthetical) are present in part6.html at the expected locations and arithmetically correct (BOU range 3.0–7.6 µGal from −5.0/−12.8 nT; W004 range 6.1–15.6 µGal from −10.3/−26.2 nT; 22.24/15 = ~1.5× upper edge; 15/1.677 = 8.94 µGal). The structural argument (two-horned vise → both horns lose a WIN) is preserved. The patches strengthen the section's epistemic posture by trading 'unpublished and unverifiable' for 'published as W004, defensible on the merits, and over-precise relative to its empirical basis.' The single moderate hole introduced is the ±2 µGal/±2 nT unit confusion — the qualitative conclusion (shortfall disappears under station uncertainty) still holds because 1.06 µGal < propagated ±1.19 µGal, but the citation must be tightened. Two minor issues remain: paragraph density (~600 words now), and the 'high-coverage central stations' selection criterion is loose. These are post-patch improvements available, not patch failures. Recommend minor_edit for the ±2 µGal/nT fix; the other two are optional polish.

citations_verified: Kim & Chang 2018 Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 (5–15 nT ensemble) — anchor present in section text; consistent with patch citation.
raw-text/03-predictions.txt baseline values BOU −10.9 nT and W004 −22.24 nT — verified at line 23 of clone HEAD d905eac1.
Per-station BOU/W004 ranges in patch (−5.0/−12.8 nT BOU; −10.3/−26.2 nT W004) — verified against raw-text/03-predictions.txt lines 26–34.
κ=1.677 nT/µGal — verified against monitor.py KAPPA constant and section text 'derivation' subsection.
citations_failed: '±2 µGal per-station uncertainty bands the dome publishes' — citation is unit-wrong. Dome publishes ±1.0 to ±2.6 nT (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:26–34), not µGal. Through κ this propagates to ±1.19 µGal, not ±2 µGal.
citations_unchecked: Mansinha et al. 2001 J. Geodet. Soc. Japan 47(1) 387–391 DOI 10.11366/sokuchi1954.47.387 (1999 Membach SG null) — present in section, not re-verified this run (pre-existing, not affected by H1-H5 patches).

Strestík (2001), Hvožďara & Prigancová (2002), Curto et al. (2006) — 1999 European eclipse magnetic observations cited in §6.14 'What κ predicts during past eclipses' subsection; not affected by H1-H5 patches.
